top_signals.title: States Take Trump to Court Over Offshore Wind Cancelations
top_signals.summary: The Trump administration’s war on wind is heading back to court. New York’s attorney general sued the administration Tuesday over one of its deals to end an offshore wind project. Under the deal announced in March, French company TotalEnergies is receiving nearly $1 billion to walk away from two US offshore wind leases off the coasts of New York and North Carolina. Associated Press reports: State attorneys general from Maine, Massachusetts, New Jersey, Rhode Island and Vermont joined New York in challenging the cancelation of the lease…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: White House unveils pared-back AI executive order
top_signals.summary: The order notes that federal access to the models should be subject to “appropriate confidentiality, cybersecurity, insider-risk, and intellectual-property protection, use, and nondisclosure requirements.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
